column2: state each system's primary function

column1: #    and give one common disorder associated with each."
# Plus the stub's "no system works alone" bullet
column2: anchored by three
alveolus
baroreceptor
column3: etc.) belongs in the per-system deep-dive

column2: definition
A group of organs that work together to carry out a shared function.
column4: The eleven organ systems are the building blocks of human anatomy.

column2: The body's ability to keep its internal conditions stable despite changes outside.
column4: Homeostasis breaks down when the body can't cool itself during heatstroke.

column2: The organ system of skin, hair, and nails; provides barrier, thermoregulation, and sensation.
column4: The integumentary system is the body's first line of defense against infection.

column2: The organ system of bones, joints, and cartilage; provides support and protection and stores calcium.
column4: The skeletal system does more than hold you up — it also stores calcium.

column2: The organ system of skeletal, smooth, and cardiac muscle; generates movement and body heat.
column4: The muscular system generates movement, posture, and body heat.

column2: The organ system of heart, blood, and vessels; transports oxygen, nutrients, and waste.
column4: The cardiovascular system delivers oxygen to every cell and carries waste away.

column2: The organ system of lymph vessels, nodes, spleen, and thymus; manages fluid balance and immune defense.
column4: The lymphatic system returns leaked fluid to the bloodstream and fights infection.

column2: The organ system of airways and lungs; performs gas exchange between air and blood.
column4: The respiratory system moves oxygen into the blood and carbon dioxide out.

column2: The organ system that breaks down food and absorbs nutrients; includes GI tract plus liver, pancreas, and gallbladder.
column4: The digestive system stretches from the mouth to the anus.

column2: The organ system of kidneys, ureters, bladder, and urethra; filters blood and regulates fluid balance.
column4: The urinary system filters blood and regulates fluid balance.

column2: The organ system of hormone-producing glands; provides chemical regulation across the body.
column4: The endocrine system regulates growth, metabolism, mood, and reproduction.

column2: The organ system of brain, spinal cord, and peripheral nerves; coordinates control and communication.
column4: The nervous system carries electrical signals that coordinate every other system.

column2: The organ system of gonads and accessory structures that produces offspring and sex hormones.
column4: The reproductive system also releases hormones that shape puberty.

column2: A process that requires two or more organ systems to work together.
column4: Regulating blood pressure is a system interaction between cardiovascular, renal, endocrine, and nervous systems.

column2: Chronically high blood pressure; a cross-system disorder affecting cardiovascular and urinary regulation.
column4: Untreated hypertension damages the heart, kidneys, and eyes over years.

column2: A group of conditions marked by high blood glucose; damages endocrine, urinary, nervous, and cardiovascular systems over time.
column4: Diabetes mellitus raises the risk of kidney, nerve, eye, and heart disease.

column2: Brain injury from loss of blood flow (ischemic) or bleeding (hemorrhagic); a disorder that straddles cardiovascular and nervous systems.
column4: A stroke is a cardiovascular event that produces a nervous-system injury.
